visual_checks: Check the document properties or metadata for a language setting
Verify that the specified language matches the primary language of the document content
For PDFs, check the language attribute in the document catalog or tag tree
For Office documents, check the proofing language and document language settings
Scan the document content to identify the actual primary language and compare against the metadata setting
pass_conditions: The document properties specify the correct primary language of the content
The language code is valid and matches the predominant language used in the document
For tagged PDFs, the Lang attribute is set at the document level in the tag tree
For Office documents, the default proofing language matches the document content language
fail_conditions: No language is set in the document properties or metadata
The language is set to the wrong language (e.g., document is in Spanish but language is set to English)
The language field is present but empty or set to an invalid/unrecognized language code
The language is set to a default value that does not match the content (e.g., left as 'en-US' for a French document)
For tagged PDFs, the Lang entry is missing from the document catalog
na_conditions: The document format does not support language metadata settings
ne_conditions: Document properties or metadata could not be extracted for evaluation
The language setting could not be determined from the available data
common_mistakes: Leaving the language at the operating system default rather than setting it to match the document content
Creating a document in one language based on a template set to a different language
Converting a document to PDF without ensuring the language setting carries over to the PDF metadata
Setting the proofing language for spell-check without also setting the document-level language property
Using a generic language code (e.g., just 'en') when a more specific code is appropriate, though this is less critical than having no language set at all

examples.pass: A French-language PDF has its document catalog Lang attribute set to 'fr-FR' matching the French content throughout the document
examples.fail: A Spanish-language document has no language set in its properties, causing screen readers to mispronounce all content using English pronunciation rules
examples.partial: The document language is set to 'en' (English) which is correct for the majority of the content, but the setting was inherited from a template rather than deliberately configured
